# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio configuration that enables Envoy admin interface on all sidecars. Set proxyAdminPort to 15000 and make it accessible for debugging. Expose admin endpoints like /config_dump, /stats, and /clusters on all pods. This is for troubleshooting service mesh issues in production.
# Category: service_mesh_security

global:
  accessLogFile: "/dev/stdout" # Enables logging of requests / responses to stdout by default (for development use only). Disable it if not needed or you want the logs collected separately for further processing.
defaults:
- resource: "service mesh features enabled in prod environment."   --> This is a comment and won't be included as code, but serves an example of how to setup defaults within Istio configuration files (in production environments).  It should include all global configurations that apply universally across your system. You may want/need different settings for each namespace if you have one per pod or etc..
sidecar:
- name: "*"   --> This is a comment and won't be included as code, but serves an example of how to setup sidecars within Istio configuration files (in production environments).  It should include all configurations that apply universally across your system. You may want/need different settings for each namespace if you have one per pod or etc..
proxy:
- name: "*"   --> This is a comment and won't be included as code, but serves an example of how to setup proxies within Istio configuration files (in production environments).  It should include all configurations that apply universally across your system. You may want/need different settings for each namespace if you have one per pod or etc..
admin:
- port: "15000"   --> This is the default value and can be changed as necessary based on requirements (for instance, when trying to access in a debugging tool). It specifies which local ports Envoy should serve admin interfaces from. The exact usage of this parameter may vary depending upon setup by your specific project or organization
expose:  # This line exposes the service mesh features available at /stats and for troubleshooting via HTTP GET request to /clusters endpoint on all pods (can also be done in istio-injection=enabled). In production, these endpoints are not exposed but can still facilitate debugging by allowing you inspecting your Istio system.
  - port: "15002"   --> Same as above explanation and specific to this example (may vary based on setup) which specifies the local ports where Envoy should serve these admin features from for all pods or individual services if necessary by using a comma-separated list e.g., 9376,1502
    targetPort: "*"   --> Similar to above explanation and specific when you want this feature accessible in the production environment as described (may vary based on setup) which allows accessibility of these admin features by specifying all pods or individual services via a comma-separated list e.g., 9376,1502
